subject: grammar
title: Present Perfect vs Past Simple
instruction: Tick (✓) the correct underlined verbs, and correct the verbs that are wrong. Write ✓ if the verb is correct, or write the correct form if it is wrong. Do not write the full sentence, only the correct form of the verb.
passage:
I would like to be considered for your degree course in Zoology, starting in October next year. I feel I am a good candidate for this course as I have always been interested in natural history, and even as a child I enjoyed studying animals and insects in my garden. Your science faculty has a good reputation and I would very much like to be part of it.

As you <u>already saw</u> [3] in Section A of this application, I have a good academic record and I <u>just received</u> [4] the results of my recent exams, all of which <u>have been</u> [5] excellent.

In addition, your university attracts me because I enjoy sports and I <u>have read</u> [6] in your prospectus about the large number of sports on offer. Last year I <u>have represented</u> [7] my school at badminton and I <u>played</u> [8] in football teams since I was eleven. I <u>have recently joined</u> [9] a basketball team which competes at a national level.

I <u>did not travel</u> [10] abroad much yet although as a young child I <u>have been</u> [11] to Singapore and Hong Kong with my family. I realize that I <u>have not spent</u> [12] much time away from home up to now, but am keen to become more independent.
